tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent rises to $104.73/bbl on Mar 16 as Dubai Airport drone strike, Fujairah fire, and UAE missile attacks on Day 17 widen Iran's target set to Gulf civilian infrastructure. Trump's warship coalition drew zero public commitments from Japan, China, France, or South Korea — removing a key de-escalation catalyst. Iran's selective-access framing is not a reopening. Hold core long; working range is $100-120 and stress case $125+ if Fujairah attacks escalate. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent is at $104.73 on Mar 16 with Dubai Airport and Fujairah attacks confirming Gulf infrastructure is now in scope. ASEAN Energy Equities proxy is 148.7 (+1.8%), while ASEAN Banks proxy is flat-to-down at 197.7 as inflation and subsidy risk compounds. Rebuild energy exposure on producers; fade rate-sensitive domestic banks losing the most if oil stays above $100. Keep the position paired rather than outright because Iran's selective-access bilateral transit framing can still trigger violent intraday squeezes on any genuine ceasefire headline.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Renewed Oil Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP weakened to 59.72 on Mar 16 (+1.2% from Mar 13) as Dubai Airport and UAE attacks extend the oil-shock horizon for Philippine fuel imports. The brief PHP relief from Pezeshkian's ceasefire feeler has fully reversed — Iran FM on Mar 16 flatly stated 'we never asked for a ceasefire.' Brent at $104.73 keeps local fuel-inflation optics worsening. Retain the 60.00-61.00 stress target; stop discipline only if a credible accepted ceasefire appears. Alliance muted response on coalition reduces any near-term military resolution catalyst.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Oil Import Stress Back in Play
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR is at 16,937 on Mar 16 while Indonesia 10Y has edged up to 6.74% (est.) — Bank Indonesia is in intervention mode not easing mode. Brent at $104.73 maintains the structural oil-import pressure on IDR. Dubai Airport attack and Fujairah fire on Mar 16 confirm Gulf hub infrastructure risk is real and extends the disruption premium. Maintain the short-IDR bias. Near-term stress target remains 17,200-17,500 with a tail scenario back above 18,000 if the maritime and infrastructure war broadens.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: The iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG proxy widened to ~163bp on Mar 16 (+3bp, est.) and the ASEAN HY composite is at ~510bp (+5bp, est.) as Dubai Airport and UAE attacks extend credit risk premium. Hold protection. The market will not re-tighten sustainably until both P&I cover returns AND Gulf infrastructure attacks cease — two conditions not yet in sight. If Brent breaks and holds above $110, 175-190bp remains credible; trim only if escorted flows normalise and oil re-prices below $90 for several sessions.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Keep Optionality
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM carried at $16.18/MMBtu (last confirmed Mar 13) versus TTF at €51.11/MWh (Mar 16 confirmed). Both legs grinding higher as Dubai Airport and Fujairah attacks broaden the disruption footprint beyond shipping to Gulf hub infrastructure. Qatar partial restart continues; US LNG at capacity. The JKM-over-TTF relative long remains for optionality. If Gulf outages spread or India/China bilateral transit deals close off the limited available flows for other buyers, JKM can reprice sharply higher.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window remains effectively closed. Brent at $104.73 supports upstream cashflow eventually, but iTraxx Asia IG at ~163bp and ASEAN HY composite at ~510bp keep unsecured funding shut. Dubai Airport attack on Mar 16 and no coalition forming extends the risk-premium horizon. Push immediately toward secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures. Do not wait for market normalisation while Gulf infrastructure attacks continue.

PTTEP should pre-fund now while Thailand 10Y remains at ~1.97% (est.) despite THB weakening to 32.47. Brent at $104.73 materially improves upstream EBITDA. Iran's selective-access framework and Trump coalition failure on Mar 16 mean another leg higher remains credible — which would pull Thai inflation expectations up and close the local-duration window. Execute the H2 2026 prefunding plan immediately and run a sanctions/compliance review on any Gulf-exposed JV structure in parallel.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity now. At Brent $104.73, Wilmar's bunker and feedstock burden is at the top of the March stress range with USD/SGD at 1.2782. Dubai Airport drone strike on Mar 16 widens insurance and port-access risk to Gulf hub logistics beyond just Hormuz shipping. Use the still-functional IG bank market to extend tenor and add committed liquidity before another jump in freight or sanctions screening tightens trade-finance appetite.

Hyflux should be on an urgent restructuring track. Base probability is 20% while the stress case is 55%, Brent is $104.73, and ASEAN HY is ~510bp. Dubai Airport and Fujairah attacks on Mar 16 push any near-term market normalisation further out. That combination keeps the unsecured market effectively closed and worsens power-cost pressure on desalination assets. Engage private-credit and quasi-sovereign options now; do not wait for a cleaner market window that may not arrive while Gulf infrastructure attacks continue.
